name: Authorize endpoint and currentUser middleware
outcome: GET /api/oauth2/authorize validates the request, redirects unauthenticated users to login, and completes approved requests; a currentUser middleware attaches the user from the jid cookie.
scope: authorize route handler + currentUser Hono middleware; preserves the hardcoded approval.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/oauth2/authorize with no session returns 302 to /api/login carrying the original query string
With a valid jid cookie, the request completes and redirects to redirect_uri with code and state
currentUser verifies the jid JWT, loads the user, and sets it on the Hono context; missing/invalid tokens pass through without a user
steps: src/app/oauth/current_user.ts — Hono middleware: read jid via getCookie, verify with the JWT service, load the user via UserRepository, c.set('user', user) on success; on missing/invalid token, await next() without setting a user
src/index.ts — type Hono Variables with { user?: User } and apply currentUser, replacing the express Request augmentation in src/global.d.ts
routes — GET /api/oauth2/authorize: build the OAuthRequest from c.req.raw, call validateAuthorizationRequest; if no c.get('user'), 302 redirect to /api/login with the params
routes — set authRequest.user = user and authRequest.isAuthorizationApproved = true (preserve the @todo), call completeAuthorizationRequest, return responseToVanilla
notes: Preserve the hardcoded isAuthorizationApproved=true and the redirect-to-/api/login-when-no-user behavior. Replaces CurrentUserMiddleware and the express Request augmentation in src/global.d.ts (deleted in cleanup).
